market_context.diagnostic_focus: center rebound hierarchy, bench-big siphon risk, and blowout-induced minute distortion

phase_1_game_phase_analysis.summary: Miami controlled the game and won by 17. Cleveland never established a stable interior rebound hierarchy, while Miami split center-board volume between Bam Adebayo and Kel'el Ware. The result punished Mobley over and preserved Bam under by hook.
key_distribution_notes: Miami rebounding was split between Bam Adebayo (10) and Kel'el Ware (11) with Pelle Larsson adding 5.
Cleveland boards were fragmented: James Harden 9, Donovan Mitchell 6, Dennis Schroder 6, Thomas Bryant 5, Evan Mobley only 5.
Mobley did not function as the dominant rebound vacuum despite Jarrett Allen being out.

notes: Kel'el Ware posted 11 rebounds in only 20 minutes, immediately capping Bam's ceiling and protecting the Bam under.
Cleveland's backcourt rebound share surged, especially via Harden (9) and Donovan Mitchell (6), removing the expected Mobley concentration.
Miami's efficiency and control reduced the kind of chaotic extended miss environment Mobley needed to reach double digits.
Bam's role remained strong, but Ware's bench presence created same-team center competition.
Mobley was active defensively but did not convert that into rebound capture due to shared team distribution and game flow loss.
Cleveland trailed for long stretches and had to play through scoring response sequences instead of channeling every possession through Mobley on the glass.

hits: Bam Adebayo under 10.5 rebounds was correct and won by hook.
Bench big siphon risk on Bam was real and should remain an active under modifier when Ware is involved.
misses: Evan Mobley over 9.5 rebounds failed badly, finishing with only 5.
Model appears to have over-assumed Jarrett Allen's absence would automatically consolidate rebounds to Mobley.
patch_recommendations: Do not auto-upgrade Mobley rebound overs solely off Jarrett Allen absence without validating actual rebound concentration pattern.
Apply a bench-big siphon modifier when Miami uses Kel'el Ware in a live rebounding role.
Downgrade center overs in games where opponent wing and guard rebound share are elevated enough to flatten positional board capture.

final_verdict.overall: Correct read on Bam under, incorrect consolidation assumption on Mobley over.
final_verdict.confidence_takeaway: Absence-based center boosts need stricter validation. If the rebound ecosystem remains distributed or guard-heavy, avoid force-playing the center over.
